title: The LLM Stack
subtitle: From Silicon to Agents
tagline: A ground-up field guide to building, training, aligning, serving, and reasoning with large language models.
author: An open field guide for ML/LLM engineers

chapters.title: Preface: How to Read This Book & The Map of the Stack
chapters.scope: Orient the reader. What the LLM stack is, layer by layer (hardware -> kernels -> frameworks -> training -> alignment -> RL infra -> inference -> agents). How to use the book, the interview track, notation conventions, and a one-page 'map of the whole stack' diagram in ASCII/mermaid. Set the tone: rigorous but digestible.

chapters.title: Linear Algebra for Deep Learning
chapters.scope: Vectors, matrices, tensors; matmul as the workhorse; rank, span, basis; eigen/SVD and why low-rank matters (LoRA preview); norms; matrix calculus identities used in backprop. Numpy/torch code. Interview: derive gradient of a linear layer.

chapters.title: Probability, Statistics & Information Theory
chapters.scope: Random variables, distributions, expectation/variance, Bayes; MLE/MAP; KL divergence, cross-entropy, entropy, mutual information; perplexity; why cross-entropy is THE LLM loss. Code: compute perplexity. Interview: KL vs cross-entropy, label smoothing.

chapters.title: Calculus, Optimization & Convexity
chapters.scope: Gradients, Jacobians, Hessians, chain rule; convex vs non-convex; gradient descent variants; momentum; saddle points; Lipschitz/condition number intuition. Code: GD on a toy loss surface with plots-as-data. Interview: why SGD generalizes.

chapters.title: Numerical Computing, Floating Point & Precision
chapters.scope: IEEE754, fp32/tf32/fp16/bf16/fp8 formats, dynamic range vs precision, overflow/underflow, catastrophic cancellation, stable softmax/logsumexp, Kahan summation. Why bf16 won training. Code: implement stable softmax, show fp16 overflow. Interview-critical.

chapters.title: Machine Learning Fundamentals
chapters.scope: Supervised/unsupervised/self-supervised; bias-variance; over/underfitting; regularization (L1/L2/dropout/early stop); train/val/test, cross-validation, data leakage; metrics (precision/recall/F1/AUC/calibration); the generalization puzzle. Interview breadth core.

chapters.title: Neural Networks From Scratch: MLPs & Backprop
chapters.scope: Build an MLP and backprop engine from scratch in pure numpy (micrograd-style autograd). Forward, backward, activations, init (Xavier/Kaiming), vanishing/exploding gradients, batchnorm. Full runnable from-scratch code. Interview: implement backprop on a whiteboard.

chapters.title: Automatic Differentiation & PyTorch Internals
chapters.scope: Reverse-mode autodiff, computation graphs, the tape; PyTorch autograd, leaf tensors, .grad, no_grad, custom autograd.Function; dispatcher, ATen, views vs copies, contiguity, broadcasting. Code: custom autograd Function. Interview: explain autograd.

chapters.title: GPU Architecture & The Memory Hierarchy
chapters.scope: SMs, warps, threads, CUDA cores vs Tensor Cores; HBM vs L2 vs SMEM vs registers; memory bandwidth vs compute; occupancy; A100/H100/H200/B200 specs and NVLink; arithmetic intensity. The single most important systems chapter for kernels/inference.

chapters.title: Parallel Computing & Collective Communication
chapters.scope: Processes/threads, SPMD; NCCL collectives (all-reduce, all-gather, reduce-scatter, broadcast, all-to-all); ring vs tree all-reduce; bandwidth/latency cost models; topology (NVLink/NVSwitch/InfiniBand). The substrate under all distributed training. Code: torch.distributed demo.

chapters.title: The Accelerator Landscape: TPUs, Trainium, AMD/ROCm & Gaudi
chapters.scope: A vendor-by-vendor survey of the non-NVIDIA accelerators an LLM engineer now ships on: Google TPU (v5e/v6/v7, pods, XLA/JAX), AWS Trainium/Inferentia (Neuron SDK), AMD Instinct MI300X/MI350 with ROCm+HIP, Intel Gaudi. The programming-model and portability story end to end — CUDA vs HIP/hipify, Triton-on-AMD, XLA vs eager, what ports cleanly and what doesn't — plus reading spec sheets (HBM capacity/bandwidth, FP8/FP4 TFLOPS) to pick hardware for a training or serving workload. Includes systolic-array vs SIMT intuition.

chapters.title: Tokenization: BPE, WordPiece, Unigram & Byte-Level
chapters.scope: Why subwords; BPE algorithm from scratch, byte-level BPE (GPT), WordPiece (BERT), Unigram/SentencePiece; tiktoken; vocab size tradeoffs; special tokens; multilingual & code tokenization pitfalls; tokenization's effect on math/digits. Full from-scratch BPE trainer code.

chapters.title: Embeddings & The Input Pipeline
chapters.scope: Token embeddings, weight tying, embedding matrices, the unembedding/logits; one-hot vs lookup; embedding dimension; how text becomes tensors end to end. Code: embedding layer, weight tying.

chapters.title: The Attention Mechanism From Scratch
chapters.scope: Build scaled dot-product attention from first principles: queries/keys/values, why scale by sqrt(d), softmax, the attention matrix, masking (causal/padding). Derive it as differentiable soft retrieval. Full numpy+torch from-scratch implementation. The crown-jewel chapter.

chapters.title: Multi-Head Attention, MQA, GQA & MLA
chapters.scope: Multi-head attention motivation and implementation; KV-cache cost; Multi-Query (MQA), Grouped-Query (GQA), and Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA, DeepSeek). Memory/quality tradeoffs. Code: implement MHA->GQA. Interview: why GQA.

chapters.title: Positional Encodings: Sinusoidal, Learned, RoPE & ALiBi
chapters.scope: Why transformers need position info; absolute sinusoidal, learned; relative; RoPE derivation and implementation; ALiBi; NoPE; context-length extension via RoPE scaling (NTK, YaRN, position interpolation). Full RoPE from scratch. Critical & interview-hot.

chapters.title: The Transformer Block: Norms, Residuals, MLPs & Activations
chapters.scope: Residual stream, LayerNorm vs RMSNorm, pre-norm vs post-norm, the FFN/MLP, activation functions (ReLU/GELU/SwiGLU/GeGLU), dropout, the full block wiring. Pre-norm stability. Code: a complete block. Interview: why pre-norm, why RMSNorm.

chapters.title: Building a GPT From Scratch (nanoGPT-style)
chapters.scope: Assemble everything into a working decoder-only GPT: config, blocks, weight init, forward, training loop on tiny data, sampling/generation. Complete, runnable, heavily commented from-scratch implementation in PyTorch. The capstone of Part II.

chapters.title: Architecture Variants: Encoder-Decoder, Decoder-Only & Prefix-LM
chapters.scope: BERT (encoder), T5 (encoder-decoder), GPT (decoder-only), prefix-LM; masking patterns; bidirectional vs causal; when each is used; the decoder-only convergence. Interview: BERT vs GPT, masked vs causal LM.

chapters.title: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Architectures
chapters.scope: Sparse MoE: router/gating, top-k, experts as FFNs; load balancing (aux loss, expert-choice), capacity factor, token dropping; Switch/GShard/Mixtral/DeepSeek-MoE/fine-grained+shared experts; expert parallelism preview. Code: a toy MoE layer. Hot topic.

chapters.title: Modern Architecture Improvements & Design Choices
chapters.scope: What changed from GPT-2 to Llama/Qwen/DeepSeek/Gemma: RMSNorm, SwiGLU, RoPE, GQA, QK-norm, no biases, untied/tied embeddings, depth vs width, attention sinks, logit soft-capping. A practitioner's 'modern transformer recipe'.

chapters.title: Beyond Attention: SSMs, Mamba, RWKV & Linear Attention
chapters.scope: Quadratic attention bottleneck; linear attention; state space models (S4, Mamba/Mamba-2 selective scan); RWKV; RetNet; hybrid architectures (Jamba, hybrid Mamba-Transformer). Tradeoffs vs transformers. Where the frontier is heading.

chapters.title: Diffusion & Non-Autoregressive Language Models
chapters.scope: The non-autoregressive paradigm for text: discrete/masked diffusion LMs (LLaDA, Dream), the absorbing/masked diffusion objective vs next-token prediction, iterative parallel denoising and remasking schedules, semi-autoregressive Block Diffusion, KV-cache reuse and flexible-length generation, and production systems (Mercury). Inference economics (parallel decode, tokens/sec) and quality trade-offs vs autoregressive transformers, plus bidirectional/reversal advantages. Code: a tiny masked-diffusion text sampler.

chapters.title: Pretraining Data: Sources, Crawling & The Data Pipeline
chapters.scope: Web crawl (Common Crawl), CommonCrawl->WET/WARC, curated corpora (books, code, arXiv, Wikipedia), licensing, the data 'recipe' and mixture weights; FineWeb/Dolma/RedPajama/The Pile case studies; data engineering at scale. Code: a streaming data pipeline sketch.

chapters.title: Data Cleaning, Deduplication & Quality Filtering
chapters.scope: Language ID, heuristic filters, quality classifiers, toxicity/PII removal; exact and fuzzy dedup (MinHash/LSH, suffix arrays); near-dup across docs; decontamination vs benchmarks; the impact of dedup on quality. Code: MinHash dedup from scratch.

chapters.title: The Pretraining Objective & Loss
chapters.scope: Next-token prediction, teacher forcing, the cross-entropy/NLL loss, shifting labels, loss masking, document packing, the causal mask; bits-per-byte; UL2/span corruption alternatives. Code: the exact loss computation. Interview: what does an LLM optimize.

chapters.title: Scaling Laws: Kaplan, Chinchilla & Beyond
chapters.scope: Power-law scaling of loss with params/data/compute; Kaplan vs Chinchilla compute-optimal; the N≈20*tokens rule; inference-aware/over-training; emergent abilities debate; how to plan a training run's budget. Code: fit a scaling law. Interview favorite.

chapters.title: Distributed Training I: Data Parallelism, DDP, ZeRO & FSDP
chapters.scope: Data parallelism, gradient all-reduce, DDP internals (bucketing, overlap); the memory problem (params+grads+optimizer states); ZeRO stages 1/2/3, FSDP sharding, parameter/gradient/optimizer sharding, communication tradeoffs. Code: FSDP example. Core systems chapter.

chapters.title: Distributed Training II: Tensor, Pipeline, Sequence & Expert Parallelism
chapters.scope: Tensor parallelism (Megatron column/row split, all-reduce points); pipeline parallelism (GPipe, 1F1B, interleaved, bubbles); sequence/context parallelism (Ring Attention); expert parallelism for MoE; how to combine into 3D/4D/5D parallelism. The hard, high-value chapter.

chapters.title: Megatron-LM, DeepSpeed & Parallelism in Practice
chapters.scope: How real frameworks compose parallelism; Megatron-Core, DeepSpeed (ZeRO, ZeRO-Offload/Infinity), the parallelism config space; choosing TP/PP/DP/EP degrees for a cluster; MFU/HFU; a worked configuration for a 70B run. Practitioner-focused.

chapters.title: Mixed Precision, bf16 & FP8 Training
chapters.scope: AMP, loss scaling (static/dynamic), master weights, why bf16 needs no loss scaling; FP8 training (E4M3/E5M2, per-tensor/blockwise scaling, Transformer Engine, DeepSeek FP8); numerics and stability. Code: AMP loop. Increasingly interview-relevant.

chapters.title: Optimizers: SGD, Adam, Adafactor, Lion, Muon & Shampoo
chapters.scope: SGD+momentum, Adam/AdamW derivation and bias correction, decoupled weight decay; memory of optimizer states; Adafactor; Lion; Shampoo/second-order; Muon (the newest, newsmaking optimizer) and orthogonalization. Code: implement AdamW. Interview: Adam vs SGD.

chapters.title: Learning Rate Schedules, Warmup, Batch Size & Hyperparameters
chapters.scope: Warmup rationale, cosine/linear/WSD (warmup-stable-decay) schedules, LR vs batch size scaling (linear/sqrt), critical batch size, gradient accumulation, weight decay, grad clipping; muP/maximal-update parameterization for HP transfer. Practical recipes.

chapters.title: Training Stability, Loss Spikes & Debugging Large Runs
chapters.scope: Loss spikes and divergence, causes (bad data, LR, fp issues), mitigations (skip batches, z-loss, qk-norm, embedding norm, careful init); monitoring (grad norm, activation stats); the lived experience of babysitting a big run. War stories + checklist.

chapters.title: Checkpointing, Fault Tolerance & Long-Running Jobs
chapters.scope: Sharded/distributed checkpointing, async checkpointing, save/restore optimizer+RNG state, resuming, elastic training, hardware failure rates at scale, in-memory checkpointing, determinism/reproducibility. Systems-engineering view.

chapters.title: Long-Context Pretraining & Context Extension
chapters.scope: Why long context is hard (quadratic + memory), training vs extension; RoPE scaling (PI, NTK, YaRN), continued pretraining on long docs, document packing for long context, needle-in-haystack, ring/context parallelism for training. Connects to inference KV cache.

chapters.title: Data Mixing, Domain Weighting & Curriculum
chapters.scope: Choosing the proportions of each data source (web, code, math, books, multilingual) and scheduling them over training. Proxy-model methods (DoReMi/Group-DRO), online/adaptive mixing, manual mixture ablations, upsampling vs deduplication trade-offs, and curriculum strategies (easy-to-hard, context-window scheduling, mid-training/annealing with high-quality data near the end). Code: a DoReMi-style reweighting toy.

chapters.title: Synthetic Data for Pre- and Post-Training
chapters.scope: Generating training tokens with models rather than scraping them: rephrasing/restyling the web (WRAP), textbook-style generation (Phi, Cosmopedia/FineWeb-Edu), instruction/QA-augmented pretraining, and reasoning-trace distillation for post-training. Mixing ratios of natural vs synthetic, scaling behavior, and failure modes: model collapse, distributional narrowing, contamination, and verification/filtering of generated data.

chapters.title: Continual & Domain-Adaptive Pretraining
chapters.scope: Extending an existing base model instead of retraining: learning-rate re-warming/re-decay, replay ratios to fight catastrophic forgetting, domain/language adaptation (code, medical, legal, new languages), and streaming-data scenarios. Model-growth and weight-reuse (depth/width expansion, dense-to-MoE upcycling, vocabulary/tokenizer transfer) and planning CPT compute with loss-trajectory models.

chapters.title: Training an LLM From Scratch: The End-to-End Recipe
chapters.scope: The capstone that wires every prior stage into one runnable path from raw text to a trained, sampled-from model: corpus -> tokenizer -> uint16 shards -> GPT -> AdamW + cosine schedule -> torchrun training loop with gradient accumulation and checkpointing -> monitoring (loss, grad norm, MFU) -> eval (perplexity) -> inference/sampling. Concrete configs and expectations at four scales (laptop/single-GPU/8-GPU/multi-node) plus a failure-mode playbook, linking to every deep chapter for the internals.

chapters.title: The Roofline Model & Performance Engineering
chapters.scope: Arithmetic intensity, memory-bound vs compute-bound, the roofline; FLOPs of attention vs FFN; how to estimate model FLOPs and MFU; profiling (nsight, torch profiler); the mental model behind every optimization. Code: estimate FLOPs/MFU. Foundational for Part IV.

chapters.title: FlashAttention I: IO-Awareness & The Online Softmax
chapters.scope: The attention memory bottleneck (materializing NxN); tiling, the online/streaming softmax with running max & denominator (derive it carefully), recomputation in backward, why it's exact not approximate; the IO-aware analysis. Full pseudo-code + math. Marquee chapter.

chapters.title: FlashAttention 2 & 3: Work Partitioning, Warp Specialization & FP8
chapters.scope: FA2: better parallelism over seqlen, fewer non-matmul FLOPs, warp partitioning. FA3 (Hopper): warp-specialization, producer/consumer with TMA, async, FP8 attention, incoherent processing; comparison to alternatives. Connects to GPU arch chapter.

chapters.title: Writing GPU Kernels with Triton
chapters.scope: Triton programming model (program_id, blocks, masks, pointers), a vector-add, a fused softmax, a matmul, and a simplified flash-attention kernel — all as runnable Triton code with explanation. The most hands-on kernel chapter.

chapters.title: CUDA Programming Essentials for ML Engineers
chapters.scope: CUDA kernels, grid/block/thread, shared memory, coalescing, bank conflicts, warp primitives (shuffle), a tiled matmul in CUDA C++, comparison with Triton, when to drop to CUDA. Code: tiled matmul kernel.

chapters.title: PagedAttention & KV-Cache Memory Management
chapters.scope: KV cache size math, fragmentation, the OS-paging analogy, block tables, copy-on-write for shared prefixes, the PagedAttention kernel; how this unlocks high-throughput serving. Bridges kernels and serving (vLLM).

chapters.title: Quantization I: Post-Training Quantization (GPTQ, AWQ, SmoothQuant)
chapters.scope: Quantization basics (scale/zero-point, symmetric/asymmetric, per-tensor/channel/group), outlier problem, GPTQ (Hessian-based), AWQ (activation-aware), SmoothQuant (migrating difficulty), LLM.int8(). Math + code sketch. Interview: how/why quantize.

chapters.title: Quantization II: INT4/INT8/FP8, GGUF, bitsandbytes & QAT
chapters.scope: Weight-only vs weight+activation, INT4/NF4, FP8 inference, GGUF/llama.cpp k-quants, bitsandbytes, double quantization; quantization-aware training, QLoRA's NF4; KV-cache quantization; accuracy/perf tradeoffs across the zoo.

chapters.title: Kernel Fusion, torch.compile, CUDA Graphs & Compilers
chapters.scope: Op fusion, why launch overhead matters, CUDA graphs, torch.compile (TorchDynamo/AOTAutograd/Inductor), graph breaks, the compiler stack (XLA, TVM); how compilation speeds training+inference. Code: torch.compile before/after.

chapters.title: Memory-Efficient Training: Checkpointing, Offloading & LoRA Math
chapters.scope: Activation/gradient checkpointing (recompute vs store), the activation-memory equation, CPU/NVMe offloading, selective recomputation, the full memory budget breakdown for training; why PEFT slashes memory. Practical memory accounting.

chapters.title: Supervised Fine-Tuning & Instruction Tuning
chapters.scope: From base to instruct; SFT objective, instruction datasets (FLAN, Alpaca, ShareGPT, OpenHermes), data quality > quantity (LIMA), the three-stage recipe (SFT->preference->RL); catastrophic forgetting; full vs partial finetuning. Code: an SFT loop.

chapters.title: Chat Templates, Data Formatting & Sequence Packing
chapters.scope: ChatML/Llama/Qwen templates, special/role tokens, system prompts, masking the prompt vs completion (train on completions only), multi-turn loss masking, sample packing with attention boundaries, tool-call formatting. Code: build a chat template + loss mask.

chapters.title: PEFT I: LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA & The Adapter Family
chapters.scope: Low-rank adaptation math (W+BA, rank r, alpha scaling, init), where to apply, merging; QLoRA (NF4 + paged optimizers + double quant); DoRA, rsLoRA, LoRA+, VeRA; serving many LoRAs. Full from-scratch LoRA implementation. High-value + interview.

chapters.title: PEFT II: Prompt/Prefix Tuning, IA3, Model Merging & Soups
chapters.scope: Prompt tuning, prefix tuning, P-tuning v2, IA3; model merging (linear, SLERP, TIES, DARE, task arithmetic), model soups, Frankenmerges; when merging beats finetuning. Code: TIES merge sketch.

chapters.title: The RLHF Pipeline & Reward Modeling
chapters.scope: The InstructGPT recipe end to end; preference data collection, the Bradley-Terry model, reward model training (pairwise loss), reward model evaluation/reward hacking, the actor/critic/reward/reference four-model setup. Code: BT reward loss. Cornerstone.

chapters.title: Policy Gradients & PPO for Language Models
chapters.scope: RL formalization of generation (tokens as actions), REINFORCE, baselines, advantage, GAE; PPO clipped objective, value function, KL penalty to reference, the full PPO-for-LLM loss and loop; why PPO is finicky. Heavy math + pseudocode. Marquee RL chapter.

chapters.title: Direct Preference Optimization & Its Variants
chapters.scope: DPO derivation (the RLHF objective has a closed-form optimal policy -> a classification loss), the implicit reward, beta; IPO, KTO (prospect theory, unpaired), ORPO (odds-ratio, no ref model), SimPO, CPO, the DPO-vs-PPO debate. Code: the DPO loss. Interview-hot.

chapters.title: GRPO, RLOO & Critic-Free RL
chapters.scope: Removing the value model: REINFORCE Leave-One-Out (RLOO), Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO, group-normalized advantages), the DeepSeek-R1 recipe; GRPO loss in detail, length/format rewards, recent GRPO fixes (Dr.GRPO, token-level, clip-higher). Code: GRPO. Very current.

chapters.title: RL with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) & The Reasoning Recipe
chapters.scope: Verifiable rewards (math/code/exact-match), no reward model needed; the R1-Zero phenomenon, emergent long CoT, 'aha moments'; reward design for verifiable domains, sandboxed code execution; from RLVR to general reasoning. The defining 2024-25 idea.

chapters.title: Reasoning, Chain-of-Thought & Test-Time Compute
chapters.scope: CoT prompting, self-consistency, ToT/graph-of-thought, process vs outcome reward models (PRM/ORM), search (MCTS, best-of-n, beam), test-time scaling laws, o1/R1-style long thinking, budget forcing. How 'thinking' became a scaling axis.

chapters.title: Constitutional AI, RLAIF & Self-Improvement
chapters.scope: AI feedback instead of human (RLAIF), Constitutional AI (critique+revise, a constitution), self-rewarding LMs, self-play, rejection sampling / STaR / ReST, weak-to-strong; reducing human-label cost. Where alignment data comes from at scale.

chapters.title: Distillation, Model Compression & Knowledge Transfer
chapters.scope: Knowledge distillation (soft targets, temperature), on-policy vs off-policy distillation, sequence-level KD, distilling reasoning (R1->small), pruning (structured/unstructured, SparseGPT, Wanda), speculative-decoding's draft models as distillation. Code: KD loss.

chapters.title: Reward Hacking, Over-Optimization & Alignment Failures
chapters.scope: Goodhart's law, reward over-optimization (the KL-reward frontier), reward model exploitation, sycophancy, length bias, spurious format rewards, specification gaming; mitigations (KL control, reward ensembles, RM updates). Crucial for RL practitioners.

chapters.title: The Anatomy of an RL-for-LLM System
chapters.scope: The components: actor (policy), rollout/generation engine, reward/verifier, learner/trainer, reference model, replay/experience buffer; the data flow; why RL infra is uniquely hard (generation + training in one loop, weight sync). The mental model for Part VI.

chapters.title: The Generation–Training Loop & Rollout Engines
chapters.scope: On-policy vs off-policy, the rollout phase (batched generation with vLLM/SGLang inside training), experience collection, advantage computation, minibatch updates; throughput bottlenecks (generation dominates); overlapping generation and training. Core RL-infra concept.

chapters.title: TRL: HuggingFace's RL Library
chapters.scope: TRL architecture: SFTTrainer, RewardTrainer, DPOTrainer, PPOTrainer, GRPOTrainer; how it integrates accelerate/PEFT/vLLM; config and a runnable GRPO/DPO example; strengths/limits. The most accessible entry point. Code-heavy.

chapters.title: veRL: HybridFlow & The Single-Controller Architecture
chapters.scope: veRL (volcano engine RL): the HybridFlow paper's single-controller + multi-controller hybrid, the 3D-HybridEngine, resharding between train (FSDP/Megatron) and rollout (vLLM/SGLang) layouts, placement groups, Ray; why veRL scales. The flagship modern RL framework.

chapters.title: OpenRLHF, NeMo-Aligner & Ray-Based Systems
chapters.scope: OpenRLHF (Ray + vLLM + DeepSpeed, distributed actor/critic/RM); NeMo-Aligner (Megatron-based, TRT-LLM rollouts); Ray as the orchestration substrate (actors, placement, object store); comparison of design choices across frameworks.

chapters.title: Prime-RL, Async RL & Decentralized Training
chapters.scope: Asynchronous/off-policy RL (decoupled generation and training), staleness and importance sampling corrections, Prime Intellect's prime-rl and decentralized/globally-distributed RL (INTELLECT), TOPLOC verification; pipelined async rollouts. The async frontier.

chapters.title: Colocated vs Disaggregated RL & Weight Synchronization
chapters.scope: Sharing GPUs between training and rollout (colocate, time-slice, offload) vs separate pools (disaggregate); weight sync mechanisms (NCCL broadcast, CUDA IPC, checkpoint reload), the resharding problem between training and inference parallelism layouts; latency/utilization tradeoffs. Deep infra.

chapters.title: Reward Engineering, Verifiers & Sandboxes
chapters.scope: Building reward functions: rule-based verifiers (math equivalence, code unit tests), sandboxed execution (security, isolation, throughput), LLM-judge rewards, reward shaping, multi-objective/weighted rewards, format vs correctness; verifier infra (e.g. sandboxes, reward servers). Practical.

chapters.title: Advantage Estimation, KL Control & Stability Tricks
chapters.scope: GAE vs Monte-Carlo vs group-relative advantages; advantage normalization/whitening; KL estimators (k1/k2/k3), KL in reward vs loss, adaptive KL; clipping (PPO ratio, clip-higher, dual-clip), entropy bonus, loss aggregation (token vs sequence mean), the bag of stabilization tricks. Very practical.

chapters.title: Agentic & Multi-Turn RL
chapters.scope: RL over multi-turn / tool-using / environment-interacting agents; trajectory-level credit assignment, turn-level rewards, environment design (RAGEN, ReACT-style), masking observations/tool outputs from loss, partial rollouts; the hard problems of agentic RL. Connects to Part VIII.

chapters.title: Scaling RL: Throughput, Load Balancing & The Latest Tricks
chapters.scope: Generation load imbalance (variable-length rollouts), dynamic sampling/filtering (DAPO), over-long filtering, partial rollout, continuous batching in RL, replay, curriculum, the latest published tricks (DAPO, VAPO, dr.grpo, length control) and where throughput is won. State-of-the-art roundup.

chapters.title: RL Data, Curriculum & Replay Management
chapters.scope: How prompts/tasks flow into an RL run: dataset construction and quality control for RLVR/agentic tasks, difficulty estimation and difficulty-targeted online selection (keeping prompts near ~50% pass rate), prompt curriculum, dynamic sampling to avoid zero-variance groups, and prioritized/rollout replay buffers. The data-side levers that determine RL sample efficiency and throughput. Code: a dynamic-sampling + difficulty-bucketing loop.

chapters.title: The Anatomy of LLM Inference: Prefill, Decode & The KV Cache
chapters.scope: The two phases (compute-bound prefill vs memory-bound decode), the KV cache and its size math, why decode is bandwidth-bound, tokens/sec ceilings, the latency metrics (TTFT, TPOT, ITL), Little's law for throughput. The foundation of Part VII.

chapters.title: Continuous Batching & Request Scheduling
chapters.scope: Static vs dynamic vs continuous (iteration-level) batching, Orca's insight, the scheduler, prefill/decode interleaving, priority and fairness, preemption; how continuous batching multiplies throughput. Code: a toy scheduler.

chapters.title: vLLM: Architecture, PagedAttention & Internals
chapters.scope: vLLM design: PagedAttention (recap), the block manager, the scheduler, the engine/executor, V1 architecture, prefix caching, speculative decoding support, multi-LoRA; how to actually run and tune it. The dominant OSS serving engine.

chapters.title: SGLang: RadixAttention & Structured Programs
chapters.scope: SGLang's RadixAttention (automatic KV reuse via a radix tree of prefixes), the frontend language (gen/select/fork/join), the runtime, constrained decoding, zero-overhead scheduler; comparison with vLLM. User has sglang locally — be concrete.

chapters.title: TensorRT-LLM, TGI & Other Serving Stacks
chapters.scope: TensorRT-LLM (compiled engines, in-flight batching, kernels), Text Generation Inference, llama.cpp/Ollama, LMDeploy, MLC; the tradeoff space (portability vs peak perf); when to pick which. Practitioner survey.

chapters.title: Speculative Decoding: Draft Models, Medusa, EAGLE & Lookahead
chapters.scope: The accept/reject sampling that preserves the target distribution (derive it), draft-then-verify, draft model choice, Medusa heads, EAGLE/EAGLE-2/3 (feature-level), lookahead decoding, tree attention, acceptance rate vs speedup. Code: speculative sampling. Interview-relevant.

chapters.title: Prefix Caching & KV-Cache Reuse
chapters.scope: Automatic prefix caching (hash-based block sharing), system-prompt reuse, RadixAttention vs vLLM APC, cache eviction (LRU), cross-request sharing, the economics of shared prefixes (agents, few-shot, RAG). Practical wins.

chapters.title: Disaggregated Prefill/Decode & Chunked Prefill
chapters.scope: Why separate prefill and decode (different resource profiles), KV transfer between pools, chunked prefill (split long prefills to avoid stalling decode), the prefill-decode interference problem; DistServe/Splitwise ideas. Cutting-edge serving.

chapters.title: Sampling Strategies & Decoding Algorithms
chapters.scope: Greedy, temperature, top-k, top-p/nucleus, min-p, typical, repetition/frequency penalties, beam search, contrastive/DoLa, the bias-diversity tradeoff; logit processors; how temperature interacts with RL/distillation. Code: implement samplers.

chapters.title: Structured & Constrained Generation
chapters.scope: JSON/grammar-constrained decoding via finite-state machines and pushdown automata; Outlines, XGrammar, llguidance, jsonformer; logit masking, the FSM compilation, speed (XGrammar's overlap with sampling), tool-call enforcement. Code: a regex-constrained sampler.

chapters.title: Multi-GPU & Multi-Node Inference
chapters.scope: Tensor parallelism for serving, pipeline parallelism, expert parallelism (large MoE), data parallel replicas, when each helps latency vs throughput, communication overhead in decode, wide-EP (DeepSeek). Sizing a deployment.

chapters.title: Inference Economics: Latency, Throughput & Cost
chapters.scope: The latency-throughput-cost triangle, batching vs latency SLOs, tokens-per-dollar, GPU selection, capacity planning, autoscaling, the math of $/1M tokens, why decode-heavy workloads are expensive; optimizing the bill. Ties Part VII together.

chapters.title: Serving Mixture-of-Experts: Expert Parallelism & All-to-All Inference
chapters.scope: The distinct serving problem of large sparse MoE models (DeepSeek-V3/R1, Llama-4, Qwen-MoE): expert-parallel (EP) inference, all-to-all token dispatch/combine (DeepEP), expert load balancing and hot-expert skew at decode, attention-DP + expert-EP hybrid layouts, and large-EP deployments. MoE-specific KV and expert-weight offloading, prefill-vs-decode EP differences, and the economics of dense-equivalent throughput.

chapters.title: Multi-Tenant LoRA & Adapter Serving at Scale
chapters.scope: Serving hundreds-to-thousands of LoRA adapters over a shared base model: batched heterogeneous-adapter kernels (Punica/SGMV), S-LoRA / LoRAX architectures, adapter registry, hot-swapping and tiered adapter storage, unified vs disaggregated execution, and cross-model prefix-cache reuse. Throughput/SLO trade-offs, per-tenant fairness, and the vLLM/SGLang multi-LoRA paths. Code: a batched multi-adapter forward.

chapters.title: Tool Use & Function Calling
chapters.scope: How models call tools: schemas, the function-calling fine-tune, parsing/validation, parallel tool calls, the tool-call loop, error handling, structured outputs; training for tool use. Code: a tool-calling loop. Foundation of agents.

chapters.title: The Agentic Loop: ReAct, Plan-Execute & Reflection
chapters.scope: ReAct (reason+act), the observe-think-act loop, planning (decomposition, plan-execute), reflection/self-correction, tree search over actions, when to stop; failure modes (loops, derailment). Code: a minimal ReAct agent.

chapters.title: Harness Engineering: Building a Coding Agent
chapters.scope: Anatomy of a Claude-Code/Codex-style harness: the system prompt, the tool set (read/edit/bash/search), context assembly, the agent loop, permissions, sub-agents, todo/planning, verification; what separates a good harness from a model. The user wanted this explicitly.

chapters.title: Context Engineering & Management
chapters.scope: The context window as the scarce resource: what to put in it, retrieval vs stuffing, compaction/summarization, context rot, lost-in-the-middle, structured context (files, scratchpads), token budgeting, prompt caching for agents. The discipline of context.

chapters.title: Memory Systems for Agents
chapters.scope: Short-term (context) vs long-term (external) memory, memory stores (vector, file, graph), write/read/reflect, episodic vs semantic, memory compaction, the file-as-memory pattern; how agents persist across sessions. Code: a simple memory store.

chapters.title: The Model Context Protocol (MCP)
chapters.scope: MCP architecture: hosts/clients/servers, tools/resources/prompts, transport (stdio/HTTP), why a standard protocol matters, building an MCP server, security considerations. The emerging integration standard. Code: a minimal MCP server.

chapters.title: Multi-Agent Systems & Orchestration
chapters.scope: Orchestrator-worker, debate, role specialization, handoffs, shared memory/blackboard, when multi-agent helps vs hurts, cost; frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, OpenAI Swarm); the workflow-vs-agent distinction. Patterns and anti-patterns.

chapters.title: Agent Evaluation & Benchmarks
chapters.scope: Evaluating agents: SWE-bench/SWE-bench-Verified, WebArena, GAIA, tau-bench, terminal-bench; trajectory vs outcome scoring, pass@k, harness effects on scores, reproducibility, contamination. How agentic progress is measured.

chapters.title: Prompt Engineering as Engineering
chapters.scope: Systematic prompting: instructions, few-shot, role, format control, decomposition, CoT, self-consistency, prompt optimization (DSPy, APE), evals-driven iteration, prompt caching; why this is real engineering not folklore. Practical patterns.

chapters.title: Embeddings & Representation Learning
chapters.scope: Dense embeddings, contrastive learning (InfoNCE), sentence/text embedding models, training (in-batch negatives, hard negatives), Matryoshka embeddings, instruction embeddings, pooling; evaluation (MTEB). Code: contrastive loss. Bridges to RAG.

chapters.title: Vector Databases & Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search
chapters.scope: Exact vs approximate search, the curse of dimensionality, HNSW (graph), IVF, PQ/product quantization, ScaNN, the recall-latency-memory tradeoff, vector DBs (FAISS, Milvus, etc.). Code: brute-force vs HNSW intuition. Interview: how ANN works.

chapters.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Architectures
chapters.scope: The RAG pipeline (chunk->embed->index->retrieve->generate), why RAG (grounding, freshness, citations), naive RAG and its failure modes, evaluation (faithfulness, relevance, RAGAS); the design space. Code: a minimal RAG.

chapters.title: Chunking, Reranking & Hybrid Search
chapters.scope: Chunking strategies (fixed/semantic/late chunking), hybrid search (BM25 + dense, reciprocal rank fusion), cross-encoder rerankers, query rewriting/HyDE, metadata filtering; the practical levers that make RAG work.

chapters.title: Advanced RAG: GraphRAG, Agentic RAG & Long-Context vs RAG
chapters.scope: GraphRAG (entity/community graphs), agentic/iterative retrieval, multi-hop, self-RAG/corrective RAG, contextual retrieval, the long-context-vs-RAG debate and when each wins, RAG over code/structured data. The frontier of retrieval.

chapters.title: Multimodal & Visual-Document Retrieval: ColPali & Late Interaction
chapters.scope: Retrieval when the corpus is images, screenshots, slides and scanned PDFs: CLIP/SigLIP cross-modal dense retrieval, ColBERT-style late interaction extended to vision (ColPali, ColQwen2), multi-vector indexing (PLAID/HNSW over patch embeddings), and the OCR-free 'page-as-image' RAG pipeline feeding retrieved pages to a VLM. Serving/indexing trade-offs of multi-vector embeddings, visual reranking, and evaluation (ViDoRe).

chapters.title: Vision Transformers & Image Encoders
chapters.scope: ViT (patches as tokens), CLIP (contrastive image-text), SigLIP, image preprocessing, patch embeddings, register tokens, DINOv2; how images enter the transformer world. Code: patchify + ViT block.

chapters.title: Vision-Language Models
chapters.scope: Connecting a vision encoder to an LLM: LLaVA (projector), Flamingo (cross-attn), Qwen-VL/InternVL, native-resolution/any-resolution, the visual-token explosion, training stages, OCR/document understanding. The dominant multimodal recipe.

chapters.title: Audio, Speech & Multimodal Fusion
chapters.scope: Audio tokenization (codecs, Whisper encoder), ASR/TTS, speech LLMs, audio-in/audio-out, real-time/streaming, any-to-any fusion; the multimodal token-stream view. Survey + key ideas.

chapters.title: Diffusion Models & Generative Modeling (Breadth)
chapters.scope: Diffusion (forward/reverse process, DDPM, score matching, classifier-free guidance, latent diffusion), flow matching, comparison with autoregressive; diffusion LMs; why an LLM engineer should understand it. Interview breadth. Conceptual + key math.

chapters.title: Unified & Any-to-Any Models
chapters.scope: Unified understanding+generation (autoregressive image gen, Chameleon/transfusion), mixed-modal training, tokenizing everything, the 'one model many modalities' trend; open problems. Forward-looking.

chapters.title: The Evaluation Problem & Benchmark Landscape
chapters.scope: Why eval is hard, the benchmark zoo (MMLU/MMLU-Pro, GSM8K/MATH, HumanEval/MBPP, GPQA, BBH, IFEval, AIME, ARC-AGI), contamination, saturation, multiple-choice pitfalls, the gap between benchmarks and usefulness. Critical literacy.

chapters.title: LLM-as-a-Judge & Automated Evaluation
chapters.scope: Using LLMs to grade: pairwise vs pointwise, rubric grading, position/verbosity/self-preference bias and mitigations, agreement with humans, Chatbot Arena/Elo, judge calibration; reward models as judges. Code: a judge prompt + Elo.

chapters.title: Building Eval Harnesses
chapters.scope: lm-evaluation-harness, HELM, the mechanics (prompt formatting, log-likelihood vs generation scoring, few-shot, normalization), reproducibility, building a custom eval, statistical significance / error bars. Practical.

chapters.title: Reasoning, Coding & Agentic Evals
chapters.scope: Pass@k, code execution sandboxes, math verification, reasoning-trace evaluation, agentic eval harnesses, test-time-compute-aware eval, avoiding reward hacking in evals; how frontier reasoning is measured.

chapters.title: Red-Teaming, Safety & Robustness Evaluation
chapters.scope: Safety benchmarks, jailbreak/adversarial eval, automated red-teaming, refusal vs over-refusal, bias/toxicity eval, robustness to perturbation, capability elicitation/dangerous-capability evals. The safety-eval toolkit.

chapters.title: Statistical Rigor in Evaluation: Confidence Intervals & Significance
chapters.scope: Reporting eval results like a scientist: bootstrap confidence intervals on accuracy/Elo, paired significance tests (paired t, McNemar, Wilcoxon) for A-vs-B, variance decomposition across prompts/seeds/judges, and power analysis to size a test set. Item-response theory and adaptive/efficient evaluation, and why overlapping CIs on a leaderboard mean 'no real gap.' Code: bootstrap CI + a paired test.

chapters.title: Designing an LLM Serving System
chapters.scope: End-to-end system design: gateway, load balancer, model routing, replica/cluster sizing, queueing, SLOs (p50/p99 TTFT), autoscaling on GPUs, multi-model serving, batching policy; a reference architecture. Doubles as interview system-design.

chapters.title: Observability, Logging & LLMOps
chapters.scope: Tracing requests, token/latency/cost metrics, prompt/response logging, evaluation-in-production, drift detection, A/B testing, the LLMOps lifecycle, tools (Langfuse, etc.); what to monitor and alert on.

chapters.title: Caching, Routing & Cost Control in Production
chapters.scope: Semantic/exact caching, prompt caching economics, model routing/cascades (cheap-then-expensive), speculative routing, quantized fallbacks, batching for cost, spot/preemptible GPUs; engineering the bill down.

chapters.title: Safety, Guardrails & Content Moderation
chapters.scope: Input/output guardrails, moderation classifiers, PII detection/redaction, system-prompt defenses, refusal policies, structured safety (constitutions), Llama Guard / shield models; the production safety stack.

chapters.title: Data Flywheels & Continuous Improvement
chapters.scope: Logging -> labeling -> retraining loops, preference collection in production, active learning, distillation from production traffic, eval-gated deployment, the compounding data advantage; building a self-improving product.

chapters.title: Security: Prompt Injection, Jailbreaks & Defenses
chapters.scope: Direct/indirect prompt injection, data exfiltration via tools, the lethal trifecta (private data + untrusted content + exfiltration), jailbreak taxonomy, defenses (sandboxing, least privilege, dual-LLM, input/output filtering), supply-chain risks. Essential security.

chapters.title: Online Evaluation: A/B Testing, Canaries & Guardrail Metrics
chapters.scope: Connecting offline benchmarks to live signal: designing A/B and interleaving experiments for LLM features, proxy/guardrail metrics (resolution rate, deflection, thumbs-up, regeneration) and avoiding metric gaming, sequential testing and CUPED for low-traffic products, shadow deployments and canary rollouts, and live judges scoring sampled production traffic. Offline-online correlation, novelty effects, and feedback-loop bias.

chapters.title: Reliability Engineering for LLM Systems: SLOs & Incident Response
chapters.scope: SRE adapted to LLMs: SLIs/SLOs for probabilistic systems (latency tails, availability, quality SLOs), a four-root-cause diagnosis tree (model/provider regression, prompt change, retrieval drift, upstream data), prompt/model rollback, and trace-attached postmortems. The LLM-specific failure of gradual silent quality collapse, fallback/degradation design, provider-outage runbooks and multi-provider failover.

chapters.title: Mechanistic Interpretability & Model Internals
chapters.scope: Looking inside a transformer: the residual-stream/superposition view, probing, the logit lens, activation patching and causal tracing, circuit discovery, and sparse autoencoders (SAEs/transcoders) for feature extraction. Practical interpretability for debugging, activation steering, feature-based monitoring and safety; tooling (TransformerLens, SAE frameworks) and honest limits. Code: logit lens + a tiny activation-patching experiment from scratch.

chapters.title: Knowledge Editing & Machine Unlearning
chapters.scope: Surgically changing what a model knows without full retraining: locate-then-edit (ROME, MEMIT, AlphaEdit), memory/adapter methods (GRACE, WISE), and failure modes (ripple effects, forgetting at scale, locality). Machine unlearning for compliance/safety (TOFU/MUSE benchmarks, gradient-ascent and editing-as-unlearning recipes), and links to right-to-be-forgotten. Code: a minimal rank-one (ROME-style) edit.

chapters.title: Privacy, Memorization & Differential Privacy for LLMs
chapters.scope: Why and when models memorize training data; extraction, reconstruction and membership-inference attacks; canary-based privacy auditing; PII leakage. Defenses across the lifecycle: data sanitization/PII scrubbing, DP-SGD and DP fine-tuning with their utility/compute trade-offs, and inference-time mitigations. Threat modeling and compliance framing (GDPR, HIPAA). Code: a membership-inference + canary-extraction demo.

chapters.title: Watermarking, Provenance & AI-Content Detection
chapters.scope: Statistical text watermarking (Kirchenbauer green-list, distortion-free schemes) and image/audio/video watermarking (SynthID-class), and their robustness to paraphrase/removal attacks. Content-provenance standards (C2PA content credentials, signed manifests), the limits of post-hoc AI-text detectors, and the compliance landscape (EU AI Act Article 50 transparency). Code: a green-list watermark embedder + detector from scratch.

chapters.title: AI Safety: Scalable Oversight, Dangerous-Capability Evals & Frontier Safety
chapters.scope: The technical safety agenda beyond RLHF: scalable oversight (debate, recursive reward modeling, weak-to-strong generalization), dangerous-capability and misuse evaluations, deception/sandbagging and reasoning faithfulness, the control-vs-alignment distinction, and frontier-safety frameworks / responsible scaling policies. How labs operationalize 'is this model safe to deploy.' Cross-links to evaluation and red-teaming.

chapters.title: AI Governance, Compliance & Regulation
chapters.scope: The regulatory and governance layer engineers must implement: the EU AI Act's obligations and timeline (GPAI duties, enforcement/fines from Aug 2026, the 1e25-FLOP systemic-risk threshold, technical documentation and training-data summaries), risk tiers and high-risk requirements, plus model cards, datasheets, transparency/eval reporting, copyright/provenance, serious-incident reporting and audit trails. NIST AI RMF and ISO/IEC 42001 as complementary frameworks.

chapters.title: Glossary of Terms
chapters.scope: A comprehensive A-Z glossary of the entire stack's terminology, each with a one-to-three sentence definition and cross-links to the chapter that covers it. 200+ entries.

chapters.title: The Math Reference Sheet
chapters.scope: All the key equations in one place: softmax, cross-entropy, attention, RoPE, LayerNorm/RMSNorm, AdamW, PPO/GRPO/DPO losses, KL estimators, scaling laws, LoRA — with one-line reminders of meaning. A printable cheat sheet.

chapters.title: Key Papers: An Annotated Reading List
chapters.scope: The canonical papers organized by topic (architecture, training, alignment, RL, inference, agents), each with a 2-3 sentence 'what it introduced and why it matters'. A curated curriculum.

chapters.title: Tooling & Environment Setup Cheatsheet
chapters.scope: The practitioner's toolbox: PyTorch, transformers, datasets, accelerate, deepspeed, vLLM, SGLang, TRL/veRL, flash-attn, bitsandbytes, wandb; environment/CUDA setup, common gotchas, useful commands. Hands-on reference.

chapters.title: From-Scratch Code Index
chapters.scope: A master index of every from-scratch implementation in the book (autograd, BPE, attention, RoPE, GPT, LoRA, DPO, GRPO, flash-attn pseudocode, speculative decoding, RAG, samplers), with pointers and a note on how they fit together.
